title: centrolepis strigosa subsp. pulvinata
intro: Centrolepis strigosa subsp. pulvinata is a plant species that belongs to the sedge family (Centrolepidaceae) and is native to New Zealand. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant's medicinal properties are said to provide several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting healing. In herbal preparations, Centrolepis strigosa subsp. pulvinata is often used in infusions, decoctions, or as a topical application, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
